_doc: Reference hook config for the test-gate agent. Copy the 'hooks' block below into your .claude/settings.local.json under the top-level object. CC self-modification rail prevents agents from writing to settings.local.json directly, so this is the manual copy step.
_install_steps: 2. Add a top-level 'hooks' key (or merge into an existing one) with the content under 'hooks' below
3. Save. The hook fires on the next Claude Code session.
_what_the_hook_does: Intercepts EVERY Bash tool call whose command matches `pytest|hardhat test|forge test`.
AFTER the tool runs (PostToolUse), checks the exit code from $CLAUDE_TOOL_EXIT.

If non-zero AND it looks like a real test failure, emits a structured prompt that Claude treats as user input — instructing Claude to invoke the test-gate subagent on the failing command for self-healing classification.
The hook itself is a single bash one-liner; the workaround logic lives in the test-gate agent.

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_COMMAND" | grep -qE '(pytest|hardhat[[:space:]]+test|forge[[:space:]]+test)' && [ "${CLAUDE_TOOL_EXIT:-0}" -ne 0 ]; then printf '[test-gate-hook] FAILED pytest/hardhat detected (exit %s). Re-dispatch via test-gate subagent to classify pass/fail/known-friction. Spec at .claude/agents/test-gate.md. Failing command: %s' "$CLAUDE_TOOL_EXIT" "$CLAUDE_TOOL_COMMAND"; fi
